description: Google Chat webhook URLs, app/bot credentials, and chat-specific admin red flags. Bot OAuth is shared with the broader GWS skill — see google_workspace.json for that.

token_patterns.name: Google Chat Incoming Webhook URL
token_patterns.notes: The `key=` query parameter is the secret. Anyone who fetches this URL can post into the target space — treat as a credential, not a config value.

misconfigurations.title: Google Chat incoming webhook URL committed to a repo / CI variable
misconfigurations.rationale: Webhook URLs are bearer-equivalent. Public-repo leaks lead to immediate channel-spam abuse.
misconfigurations.fix: Store in a secrets manager. If leaked, regenerate the webhook from the space settings — old URL is permanently revoked.

misconfigurations.title: Google Chat app installable by external users (not domain-restricted)
misconfigurations.rationale: Cross-domain Chat apps can be social-engineered into exfiltrating data via DM to a bot.
misconfigurations.fix: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Google Chat > Chat apps — restrict to internal apps unless there is an explicit business case.

misconfigurations.title: External users allowed in spaces by default
misconfigurations.rationale: External-user-in-space is a documented exfiltration channel for confidential data.
misconfigurations.fix: Admin Console > Apps > Google Workspace > Google Chat > External chat settings — default to 'Off' and require admin approval per space.

admin_red_flags.title: Chat app message volume spike to a single space
admin_red_flags.rationale: Compromised webhooks are often used for crypto/scam spam.
admin_red_flags.signal: Reports > Chat: messages per (space, app) > 10× 30-day baseline.

admin_red_flags.title: External user added to an internal-only space
admin_red_flags.rationale: Misclick or social-engineering vector for data exfiltration.
admin_red_flags.signal: Audit log: `ADD_MEMBER_TO_SPACE` where added member email domain ∉ org domains.
